channel.title: Posts on Abhinav Srivastava / @abhinavmir
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Abhinav Srivastava / @abhinavmir

item.title: A caveman introduction to Yield Farming
item.description: “But you know what I like more than materialistic things? Knowledge.” - Tai Lopez A wise man once said: “Days are sunny, for those who HODL their money”. Okay, I made it up but looking at my career trajectory, you’re free to quote it to my name once I make it big. Unfortunalely, HODLing is on hold with the new entrants: Liquidity Pools and Yield Farming. In Summary: Yield Farming is a way to provide liquid to a certain liquidity pool, and be rewarded for it.

item.title: Bitcoin and the claim of Total Turingness [OLD]
item.description: Craig Wright is a very controversial man — Bitcoin SV, Claims of being Satoshi Nakamoto, major plagiarism accusations- Overall, not the face of Blockchain you’d want. But Craig Wright is also a very smart man, much more than the Twitter handles in the Crypto Community would have you believe. His degrees and years of experience are something you cannot ignore, or at least that’s what the BSV community and Wright himself want you to believe.

item.title: Bitcoin, Failing and Moving out - Anecdotes
item.description: I’ve been an advocate of moving out as an instrument of growing for a few years now. Of course, I come from some place of privilege: Two loving parents and financial, mental and bodily capability. Please weigh your own situation before moving out. I first moved out from North India to South India to study Computer Science. The University I attended is an authoritative hellhole. That did not sit well with me, and I mentally checked out from the Uni, focusing on building things, studying on my own and having my own experiences.

item.title: Building Iroha from Source on your Linux System
item.description: Hyperledger Iroha is a Blockchain platform written in C++ that uses YAC Consensus. Iroha mainly helps create, verify and manage fungible assets and user accounts. Installing Iroha with Docker is a lot easier, but for a lot of purposes, installing Iroha natively makes a lot more sense. Pre-requisites The first thing to do would be to update package lists. apt-get update Remember that while I’m using sudo, you should only use it after ensuring that it’s A-ok to give root permissions to install the modules required.

item.title: Gameboy -- Everything under the hood.
How does an Ethereum transaction take place
item.description: A Blockchain is essentially a public database in which the data is shared and synchronized across multiple nodes connected in a network. A Blockchain is built up of Block Data structures arranged in a Chain like cryptographic sequence. Ethereum is no different, when you commit any transaction on the chain, the data must be added to the network for it to be successful. The verification process is similar to Bitcoin, which uses Proof of Work.

item.title: How to be a Chad - Some advice for Indian Engineering Undergrads from a final year
item.description: I am not going to do disclaimers about my advice, take it or leave it, your life your choice. I’m putting this out there for you and for me. I promised a thread earlier in a tweet. Think of this as a tweetstorm where I talk about some ideas I’ve accumulated over the years. This is job-focused. I’ve done research, GRE/TOEFL, startups and MS applications too, but there are people who can tell you about that better.

item.title: Introduction to Interblockchain Communication and the Cosmos SDK
item.description: What is Interblockchain Communication? Today there are thousands of networks, permissioned and permissionless. These networks work great on their own, but to realize their true potential, we need communication between Blockchains. From Atomic Swaps to Shared Contracts, there is a lot of potential. Interblockchain Communication are just a set of protocols that allow for communication between Blockchain networks. Cosmos is a decentralized network of independent parallel blockchains, each powered by BFT consensus algorithms like Tendermint consensus.

item.title: Kanye West taught me how to live
item.description: I’ve never had a harsh childhood. My parents were very good parents, God Bless, and have always wanted the best for me. There’s been a fair share of living the typical Asian kid lifestyle: Curfews to return home, focus on academics, submission to authority — but in hindsight, it came from a place of love. Discipline, Peace, and Respect are what I’d call the pillars of Asian or at least Indian style of parenting.

item.title: Linux Cheat Sheet
item.description: The Linux Beginner’s Guide What is Linux Linux is a family of Unix-like OSs based on the Linux Kernel. Linux Kernel is a monolithic kernel with a modular design. Here is a a simplified architecture of the Linux Operating System. When you boot up your own Linux Distro system (more on this later), you are utilizing the User Space which is were your User Applications and glibc (Used for implementing functions such as fopen(), calloc(),open()) exist.

item.title: The journey of a C++ program
item.description: If you couldn’t already tell, I love Bjarne, and by extension, I love C++. In this article, I go over how C++ compiles a program to binaries, and why I love C++. This is partially to understand how C++ works. Documentation helps me understand. There are only two kinds of languages: the ones people complain about and the ones nobody uses. ― Bjarne Stroustrup, The C++ Programming Language I got pretty inspired by HaoranWang’s CRUSTand thus wanted to write my own Compiler for C.
